sections.subtitle: Current Form
sections.text: Athletic Club have shown mixed results in their last 5 matches, with 2 wins, 1 draw, and 2 losses, including a heavy 4-0 defeat to Barcelona. Real Madrid, on the other hand, remain unbeaten in their last 4 matches, securing 2 wins and 2 draws, with a high-scoring 4-3 victory over Olympiacos in the Champions League.

sections.subtitle: Head-to-Head Record
sections.text: In the last two encounters between these sides, results are split with each team securing a 1-goal victory at home (Athletic Club 2-1 in 2024, Real Madrid 1-0 in 2025). Historically, matches tend to be low-scoring, with 7 of the last 8 games producing under 2.5 goals.

sections.subtitle: Key Players and Stats
sections.text: Athletic Club will rely on Nico Williams, who has 3 goals and strong dribbling stats (2.5 per game), while Real Madrid's Kylian Mbappé is in top form with 14 goals this season and 5 in his last 5 matches. Real Madrid's superior attacking output (29 goals scored vs. Athletic's 14) and possession stats give them an edge.

sections.subtitle: Tactical Insights
sections.text: Athletic Club excels in pressing (2nd in possessions won in the final third) and penalty area entries (2nd in LaLiga), but struggles with conversion (34.5% of big chances). Real Madrid's ability to dominate possession (61% avg. in recent games) and create chances could exploit Athletic's defensive vulnerabilities (17 goals conceded).

sections.subtitle: Match Outlook
sections.text: Given Real Madrid's superior league position (2nd with 33 points vs. Athletic's 8th with 20), current form, and attacking firepower, they are favored to win. Sofascore's prediction (63% away win) aligns with their historical edge in this fixture, though Athletic's home advantage at San Mamés could make it competitive.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Half-time overview
halftimeSections.text: At the break, Real Madrid holds a commanding 2-0 lead over Athletic Club at San Mamés. The away side has been dominant in possession and clinical in attack, with goals from Kylian Mbappé and Eduardo Camavinga showcasing their quality. Athletic Club, on the other hand, has struggled to convert their limited opportunities, missing a big chance and failing to truly test Real Madrid’s defense consistently.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Attack momentum analysis
halftimeSections.text: The first half saw Real Madrid dominate the attacking momentum for significant periods, particularly in the early stages where they established control and pressed high up the pitch. Athletic Club had brief spells of pressure, especially around the middle of the half, but struggled to maintain consistent attacking intent. Real Madrid regained momentum towards the end of the half, culminating in their second goal, which further highlighted their ability to capitalize on attacking surges.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Key performers
halftimeSections.text: For Real Madrid, Kylian Mbappé has been exceptional, contributing both a goal and an assist, proving to be a constant threat. Eduardo Camavinga also stood out with his headed goal, adding to the away side’s dominance. On the Athletic Club side, Nico Williams has shown creativity with key passes and big chance creation, while Gorka Guruzeta has been their most dangerous outlet in front of goal, though without reward so far.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Tactical observations
halftimeSections.text: Real Madrid’s high possession and ability to win duels, particularly in the air, have allowed them to control the game and create numerous big chances. Their entries into the final third and penalty area have been frequent, often resulting in dangerous opportunities. Athletic Club has shown some intent in the final third but lacks the precision and composure needed to break through Real Madrid’s solid defensive structure. The home side’s lower duel success rate suggests they are being physically outmatched.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Second half outlook
halftimeSections.text: Athletic Club will need to come out with renewed energy and focus to overturn the deficit, likely pushing for an early goal to shift the momentum. Players like Nico Williams and Álex Berenguer could be key in unlocking Real Madrid’s defense. However, Real Madrid’s attacking firepower and confidence make them favorites to maintain control. They may look to manage the game but could exploit any defensive lapses from the home side on the counter. Expect Athletic Club to take more risks, potentially opening up spaces for Real Madrid to capitalize.
